definitions.definition: Reserved bits in the fixed header of the PUBREL Control Packet.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: MUST be set to 0,0,1 and 0 respectively
definitions.evidence: Bits 3,2,1 and 0 of the fixed header in the PUBREL Control Packet are reserved and MUST be set to 0,0,1 and 0 respectively.

definitions.variable_name: Bits 3,2,1 and 0 of the fixed header
definitions.definition: Reserved fixed-header bits of the UNSUBSCRIBE Control Packet.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: MUST be set to 0,0,1 and 0 respectively
definitions.evidence: Bits 3,2,1 and 0 of the fixed header of the UNSUBSCRIBE Control Packet are reserved and MUST be set to 0,0,1 and 0 respectively.

definitions.definition: Bit 1 of the Connect Flags byte; specifies the handling of the Session state.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: 0 or 1
definitions.evidence: Position: bit 1 of the Connect Flags byte. This bit specifies the handling of the Session state. If CleanSession is set to 0, the Server MUST resume communications with the Client based on state from the current Session

definitions.definition: Payload field specifying a unique Client identifier for the Client.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: Length 0 to 23+ UTF-8 encoded bytes; Server MUST allow 1 to 23 UTF-8 encoded bytes containing only "0123456789abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ"; zero-byte ClientId may be allowed by Server as a special case
definitions.evidence: The payload contains one or more encoded fields. They specify a unique Client identifier for the Client

definitions.definition: Return code field in the CONNACK response sent by the Server.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: 0x01 (unacceptable protocol level)
definitions.evidence: The Server MUST respond to the CONNECT Packet with a CONNACK return code 0x01 (unacceptable protocol level)

definitions.definition: Byte 1 of the CONNACK variable header; bits 7-1 are reserved and MUST be set to 0, and bit 0 contains the Session Present Flag.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: Bits 7-1 = 0; bit 0 used for Session Present
definitions.evidence: Byte 1 is the "Connect Acknowledge Flags". Bits 7-1 are reserved and MUST be set to 0. Bit 0 (SP1) is the Session Present Flag.

definitions.definition: CONNECT variable header field containing parameters specifying MQTT connection behavior and indicating presence or absence of fields in the payload.
definitions.evidence: The Connect Flags byte contains a number of parameters specifying the behavior of the MQTT connection. It also indicates the presence or absence of fields in the payload.

definitions.definition: Byte 2 in the CONNACK variable header; contains the return code indicating connection acceptance or refusal reason.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: 0x01-0x05 listed in this chunk; zero return code also referenced for successful acknowledgement
definitions.evidence: Byte 2 in the Variable header. The values for the one byte unsigned Connect Return code field are listed in

definitions.type: most significant bit of each Remaining Length byte
definitions.definition: Indicates that there are following bytes in the Remaining Length representation.
definitions.evidence: The least significant seven bits of each byte encode the data, and the most significant bit is used to indicate that there are following bytes in the representation. Thus each byte encodes 128 values and a "continuation bit".

definitions.definition: Duplicate delivery of a PUBLISH Control Packet.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: 0 = first occasion sent; 1 = might be re-delivery; MUST be 1 on re-delivery; MUST be 0 for all QoS 0 messages
definitions.evidence: DUP1 = Duplicate delivery of a PUBLISH Control Packet

definitions.definition: PUBLISH header flag indicating packet re-delivery/retransmission.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: Must be 1 on re-delivery; must be 0 for all QoS 0 messages
definitions.evidence: The DUP flag MUST be set to 1 by the Client or Server when it attempts to re-deliver a PUBLISH Packet. The DUP flag MUST be set to 0 for all QoS 0 messages.

definitions.definition: Byte value produced during the algorithm for encoding a non negative integer into the variable length encoding scheme.
definitions.evidence: The algorithm for encoding a non negative integer (X) into the variable length encoding scheme is as follows: encodedByte = X MOD 128

definitions.definition: Part of an MQTT Control Packet; present in all MQTT Control Packets.
definitions.module_or_section: Structure of an MQTT Control Packet
definitions.evidence: An MQTT Control Packet consists of up to three parts, always in the following order... Fixed header, present in all MQTT Control Packets

definitions.definition: Bits [3-0] of byte 1 in the fixed header containing flags specific to each MQTT Control Packet type.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: 4-bit unsigned value
definitions.evidence: The remaining bits [3-0] of byte 1 in the fixed header contain flags specific to each MQTT Control Packet type... Represented as a 4-bit unsigned value

definitions.definition: Field in the CONNECT packet variable header.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: measured in seconds
definitions.evidence: The variable header for the CONNECT Packet consists of four fields in the following order: Protocol Name, Protocol Level, Connect Flags, and Keep Alive.

definitions.definition: Prefix of each UTF-8 encoded string that gives the number of bytes in the UTF-8 encoded string itself.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: 0 to 65535 bytes
definitions.module_or_section: UTF-8 encoded strings in Control Packets
definitions.evidence: Each of these strings is prefixed with a two byte length field that gives the number of bytes in a UTF-8 encoded string itself... Unless stated otherwise all UTF-8 encoded strings can have any length in the range 0 to 65535 bytes.

definitions.definition: A common variable header field used in several MQTT Control Packet types.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: non-zero
definitions.evidence: The variable header component of many of the Control Packet types includes a 2 byte Packet Identifier field... SUBSCRIBE, UNSUBSCRIBE, and PUBLISH (in cases where QoS > 0) Control Packets MUST contain a non-zero 16-bit Packet Identifier

definitions.definition: Optional payload field whose presence is determined based on flags in the variable header.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: Present if Password Flag=1; absent if Password Flag=0
definitions.evidence: They specify a unique Client identifier for the Client, a Will topic, Will Message, User Name and Password. All but the Client identifier are optional and their presence is determined based on flags in the variable header.

definitions.definition: Indicates whether a password is present in the payload.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: Position: bit 6 of the Connect Flags byte; 0 or 1; if User Name Flag=0 then Password Flag MUST be 0
definitions.evidence: Position: bit 6 of the Connect Flags byte. If the Password Flag is set to 0, a password MUST NOT be present in the payload. If the Password Flag is set to 1, a password MUST be present in the payload. If the User Name Flag is set to 0, the Password Flag MUST be set to 0

definitions.definition: Part of an MQTT Control Packet; present in some MQTT Control Packets.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: May be zero length
definitions.module_or_section: Structure of an MQTT Control Packet
definitions.evidence: An MQTT Control Packet consists of up to three parts, always in the following order... Payload, present in some MQTT Control Packets

definitions.definition: PUBLISH Quality of Service.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: PUBLISH Packet MUST NOT have both QoS bits set to 1; Packet Identifier present only when QoS level is 1 or 2
definitions.evidence: QoS2 = PUBLISH Quality of Service

definitions.definition: PUBLISH header bits encoding QoS; both bits must not be set to 1 simultaneously.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: MUST NOT both be 1
definitions.evidence: A PUBLISH Packet MUST NOT have both QoS bits set to 1. If a Server or Client receives a PUBLISH Packet which has both QoS bits set to 1 it MUST close the Network Connection.

definitions.definition: The number of bytes remaining within the current packet, including data in the variable header and the payload, excluding the bytes used to encode the Remaining Length itself.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: 0 to 268,435,455; maximum 4 bytes encoding
definitions.evidence: The Remaining Length is the number of bytes remaining within the current packet, including data in the variable header and the payload. The Remaining Length does not include the bytes used to encode the Remaining Length... The maximum number of bytes in the Remaining Length field is four... This allows applications to send Control Packets of size up to 268,435,455

definitions.definition: Byte following each Topic Filter in the SUBSCRIBE payload; gives the maximum QoS level at which the Server can send Application Messages to the Client.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: QoS must be 0, 1 or 2; upper 6 bits reserved and must be zero
definitions.evidence: Each filter is followed by a byte called the Requested QoS. This gives the maximum QoS level at which the Server can send Application Messages to the Client. ... The upper 6 bits of the Requested QoS byte are not used in the current version of the protocol. They are reserved for future use. ... if any of Reserved bits in the payload are non-zero, or QoS is not 0,1 or 2

definitions.definition: Reserved bits in the SUBSCRIBE packet payload; must be zero.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: non-zero is invalid
definitions.evidence: The Server MUST treat a SUBSCRIBE packet as malformed and close the Network Connection if any of Reserved bits in the payload are non-zero...

definitions.definition: Reserved flag in the CONNECT Control Packet that must be set to zero.
definitions.evidence: The Server MUST validate that the reserved flag in the CONNECT Control Packet is set to zero

definitions.definition: PUBLISH Retain flag.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: 0 or 1
definitions.evidence: RETAIN3 = PUBLISH Retain flag

definitions.definition: PUBLISH header flag controlling retained-message handling.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: 0 or 1
definitions.evidence: If the RETAIN flag is set to 1, in a PUBLISH Packet sent by a Client to a Server, the Server MUST store the Application Message and its QoS... If the Server receives a QoS 0 message with the RETAIN flag set to 1... When sending a PUBLISH Packet to a Client the Server MUST set the RETAIN flag to 1 if a message is sent as a result of a new subscription being made by a Client.

definitions.definition: Field in the SUBACK Packet, one for each Topic Filter/QoS pair; indicates either the maximum QoS granted for that Subscription or that the subscription failed.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: 0x00 = Success - Maximum QoS 0; 0x01 = Success - Maximum QoS 1; 0x02 = Success - Maximum QoS 2; 0x80 and other values are mentioned only in reservation context, with codes other than 0x00, 0x01, 0x02 and 0x80 reserved and MUST NOT be used
definitions.evidence: The SUBACK Packet sent by the Server to the Client MUST contain a return code for each Topic Filter/QoS pair. This return code MUST either show the maximum QoS that was granted for that Subscription or indicate that the subscription failed

definitions.definition: Bit 0 of the Connect Acknowledge Flags in the CONNACK packet, indicating whether stored Session state is present.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: 0 or 1; MUST be 0 if CleanSession=1 accepted; MUST be 1 if CleanSession=0 accepted and stored Session state exists; MUST be 0 if no stored Session state exists; MUST be 0 when CONNACK return code is non-zero
definitions.evidence: Bit 0 (SP1) is the Session Present Flag. Position: bit 0 of the Connect Acknowledge Flags.

definitions.definition: Payload field in a SUBSCRIBE Packet; a list of Topic Filters indicates the Topics to which the Client wants to subscribe.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: Payload MUST contain at least one Topic Filter
definitions.evidence: The payload of a SUBSCRIBE Packet contains a list of Topic Filters indicating the Topics to which the Client wants to subscribe. The Topic Filters in a SUBSCRIBE packet payload MUST be UTF-8 encoded strings

definitions.definition: Identifies the information channel to which payload data is published; it is the first field in the PUBLISH Packet Variable header.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: MUST be present; MUST NOT contain wildcard characters
definitions.evidence: The variable header contains the following fields in the order: Topic Name, Packet Identifier. The Topic Name identifies the information channel to which payload data is published. The Topic Name MUST be present as the first field in the PUBLISH Packet Variable header. It MUST be a UTF-8 encoded string... The Topic Name in the PUBLISH Packet MUST NOT contain wildcard characters

definitions.definition: Optional payload field whose presence is determined based on flags in the variable header.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: Present if User Name Flag=1; absent if User Name Flag=0
definitions.evidence: They specify a unique Client identifier for the Client, a Will topic, Will Message, User Name and Password. All but the Client identifier are optional and their presence is determined based on flags in the variable header.

definitions.definition: Indicates whether a user name is present in the payload.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: Position: bit 7 of the Connect Flags; 0 or 1
definitions.evidence: Position: bit 7 of the Connect Flags. If the User Name Flag is set to 0, a user name MUST NOT be present in the payload... If the User Name Flag is set to 1, a user name MUST be present in the payload

definitions.definition: Part of an MQTT Control Packet; present in some MQTT Control Packets.
definitions.module_or_section: Structure of an MQTT Control Packet
definitions.evidence: An MQTT Control Packet consists of up to three parts, always in the following order... Variable header, present in some MQTT Control Packets

definitions.definition: Indicates whether a Will Message is stored on the Server and associated with the Network Connection.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: 0 or 1
definitions.evidence: If the Will Flag is set to 1 this indicates that, if the Connect request is accepted, a Will Message MUST be stored on the Server and associated with the Network Connection... If the Will Flag is set to 0 ... a Will Message MUST NOT be published when this Network Connection ends.

definitions.definition: Optional payload field whose presence is determined based on flags in the variable header.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: Sequence of zero or more bytes; length encoded in two bytes and excludes the two length bytes
definitions.evidence: They specify a unique Client identifier for the Client, a Will topic, Will Message, User Name and Password. All but the Client identifier are optional and their presence is determined based on flags in the variable header.

definitions.definition: Specifies the QoS level to be used when publishing the Will Message.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: Position: bits 4 and 3 of the Connect Flags; if Will Flag=0 then must be 0; if Will Flag=1 then can be 0 (0x00), 1 (0x01), or 2 (0x02); MUST NOT be 3 (0x03)
definitions.evidence: Position: bits 4 and 3 of the Connect Flags. These two bits specify the QoS level to be used when publishing the Will Message. If the Will Flag is set to 0, then the Will QoS MUST be set to 0 If the Will Flag is set to 1, the value of Will QoS can be 0 (0x00), 1 (0x01), or 2 (0x02). It MUST NOT be 3 (0x03)

definitions.definition: Specifies if the Will Message is to be retained when it is published.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: Position: bit 5 of the Connect Flags; if Will Flag=0 then must be 0; values 0 or 1
definitions.evidence: Position: bit 5 of the Connect Flags. This bit specifies if the Will Message is to be Retained when it is published. If the Will Flag is set to 0, then the Will Retain Flag MUST be set to 0... If Will Retain is set to 0... If Will Retain is set to 1...

definitions.definition: Optional payload field whose presence is determined based on flags in the variable header.
definitions.evidence: They specify a unique Client identifier for the Client, a Will topic, Will Message, User Name and Password. All but the Client identifier are optional and their presence is determined based on flags in the variable header.
